evidence.sourceText: Physician verified need for a live-in aide and separate functional sleeping arrangement.
Provider approved a unit configuration that includes a separate sleeping room for the aide.
Move-in documentation shows the aide occupies a private room and does not sleep in the living room.

authorities.label: Giebeler v. M&B Associates
authorities.notes: Accommodation rules must bend where necessary for disability-related housing access.
authorities.excerptText: Imposition of burdensome policies can interfere with disabled persons' right to use and enjoyment of their dwellings.
authorities.proposition: Burdensome housing policies may require accommodation when they interfere with use and enjoyment.

authorities.label: United States v. California Mobile Home Park Management Co.
authorities.notes: Reasonable accommodation doctrine requires case-specific assessment of accessible tenancy arrangements.
authorities.excerptText: California Mobile Home is used here as a paraphrased reminder that reasonable accommodation analysis must stay tied to how access works in practice.
authorities.proposition: Reasonable accommodation analysis must stay tied to practical housing access.

authorities.label: McGary v. City of Portland
authorities.notes: Housing-related financial burdens can impair the full use and enjoyment of a dwelling.
authorities.excerptText: The lien the City put on McGary's house prevents the full use and enjoyment of his property.
authorities.proposition: Housing-related financial burdens can impair the full use and enjoyment of a dwelling.

authorities.label: HUD/DOJ Joint Statement on Reasonable Accommodations
authorities.notes: Equal use and enjoyment requires necessary accommodation changes.
authorities.excerptText: Reasonable accommodations may be necessary to afford a person with a disability equal opportunity to use and enjoy a dwelling.
authorities.proposition: Equal use and enjoyment may require necessary accommodation changes.
authorities.citation: Joint Statement of the Department of Housing and Urban Development and the Department of Justice, Reasonable Accommodations under the Fair Housing Act (May 2004)

authorities.label: 24 CFR 982.316
authorities.notes: Program accessibility requires approval of a live-in aide when the accommodation need is established.
authorities.excerptText: The regulation is paraphrased here to support the idea that program accessibility includes approval of a live-in aide when the disability-related need is established.
authorities.proposition: Program accessibility includes approval of a live-in aide when the need is established.

notes: Contrasting fixture where the provider supplies an effective accommodation and the separate-bedroom request is satisfied in practice.
